what_it_does: Defines a single verification-juror role (`verify_lens`) that judges one change-under-test through exactly one of six named lenses, returning a structured verdict via a Pydantic model.
observations: Declares a closed six-lens vocabulary in the `LENSES` tuple.
Defines `VerifyLensOut`, a Pydantic output schema with closed verdict tokens `pass|fail|uncertain`.
The `ROLE` dict uses default `input_addresses` `('utterance',)` and expects the lens unit as the utterance payload.
Explicitly avoids `draws` and `verdict_rule`; the cross-lens tally is intended to live downstream in a reduce-rule.
Includes an `adversarial-disprove` lens that defaults to fail/uncertain if any breaking case can be constructed.
Sets `mode_scope` to `{'verification'}` as a context cast, not the listening cast.
Model binding requires `chat` and `json`, with `default_model: None`.
standouts: Axis-inversion design: one role file becomes N jurors by fanning different lens units through `run_items`.
Adversarial-disprove lens inverts the burden: pass only if no breaking case can be found.
Extensive inline rationale for why `verdict_rule` is deliberately absent.
conventions: Uses Pydantic for output schema validation.
References composition and rule identifiers (COMPOSITIONS ⑥, AGENTS.md C9.2/C1.4/C2.5).
Closed vocabularies declared as module-level constants.
concerns: The lens unit schema (`lens`/`change`/`bar`) is documented but not validated or typed in code.
No tally/reduce logic is present here; downstream integration is assumed but not referenced concretely.
Very long docstring/comments may drift from the actual implementation over time.
notes: A reusable single-lens verification juror meant to be fanned across six lenses, leaving the final verdict tally to a downstream deterministic reduce.
questions: How is the lens unit dict actually framed into `ctx['utterance']` by `run_items`?
Where is the downstream deterministic reduce-rule that tallies per-lens verdicts defined?
How does `default_model: None` resolve to a concrete model at runtime?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches; the file is a single-lens juror role designed for fan-out, exactly as the comments describe.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make one reusable verification role serve many distinct evaluative angles by parameterizing the lens in the input, enabling cheap parallel coverage while keeping verdict aggregation deterministic and separate.
novelty: A cross-lens jury composed by fanning the same role with different lens payloads, rather than drawing multiple samples from one classification role.
contribution: Provides a parameterized, single-lens verification role with a strict output schema and closed verdict vocabulary, enabling compositional multi-lens verification via `run_items` and deterministic downstream tallying.
summary_for_embedding: verify_lens.py defines a single-lens verification juror role with a Pydantic output schema and a six-lens vocabulary, intended to be fanned by run_items for cross-lens jury verification.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a reusable, advisory verification juror that judges changes through one of six lenses so multiple angles can be run in parallel and tallied deterministically.

symbols.does: Pydantic model that captures one lens judgement.
symbols.description: `VerifyLensOut` is the role's output schema. It requires the lens name, a closed verdict token (`pass`, `fail`, or `uncertain`), concrete evidence citing the change, and an optional `breaking_case` field used primarily by the adversarial-disprove lens.
